Title: CESTA, a positive regulator of brassinosteroid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: overlaps with CES; predominant in vascular tissues and early developmental stages (as stated for key BR biosynthesis genes including CPD and ROT3)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: elevated in ces-D seedlings; reduced in ces-1 seedlings; slightly reduced in 35Sp:c-Myc-CES-SRDX/203; induced by Brz2001
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cytochrome P450-catalyzed oxidation step in brassinosteroid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key biosynthetic gene, not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CPD expression correlates with increased late BR intermediates in ces-D; reduced expression correlates with BR-deficient phenotypes in CES loss/repression backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPD transcript levels were elevated in ces-D seedlings. 2. CPD expression response to Brz2001 was reduced in ces-1, indicating CES is required for full induction. 3. CES binds a G-box-containing fragment in the CPD promoter in planta and in EMSA, supporting direct regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; semi-quantitative RT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; BR metabolite profiling in CES genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream biosynthetic target for manipulating brassinosteroid levels, but not a primary core subject relative to CES in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a direct CES metabolic target repeatedly validated in Results, though the paper's main core gene is CES.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix whole-genome Arabidopsis GeneChip, accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: brassinosteroids regulate plant growth and development

Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; BR/Bikinin treatment induces relocalization to speckled subnuclear compartments
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in all organs; enriched in young tissues and vascular bundles; roots, hypocotyls, cotyledons, leaf vasculature, leaf axillary meristems, stigma; predominant in vascular tissues during early development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: overexpressed in ces-D and 2x35Sp:CES lines; absent in ces-1 full-length transcript; transgenic 35Sp:CES-YFP expressed in protoplasts/plants
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 24-epiBL; Brz2001; Bikinin; light-grown seedlings; dark-grown seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: brassinosteroid treatment; BR signaling activation by Bikinin; BIN2 kinase activity
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CPD; CYP718; positively affects DWF4; CPD; ROT3 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates BR-biosynthetic gene expression, especially CPD, DWF4 and ROT3; directly binds and activates CPD and CYP718-associated promoter regions
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; promoter binding to G-box motifs; post-translational modulation by BIN2-dependent phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: participates in BR homeostasis/feedback adjustment of BR biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms homodimers; interacts with BEE1 and BEE3 in yeast; CES-BEE1 interaction shown in planta
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CES promotes late BR accumulation and BR response phenotypes; loss or dominant repression causes BR-deficient phenotypes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive regulator required for proper expression of BR-biosynthetic genes and for balanced BR homeostasis, especially early in development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: activation-tagged mutant analysis; overexpression; loss-of-function mutant; dominant repression (CES-SRDX); BR rescue with 24-epiBL; qRT-PCR; GC/MS BR profiling; ChIP; EMSA; Y2H; BiFC; protoplast localization; in vitro kinase assay; transcriptome profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Activation tagging and 35S overexpression of CES caused BR overaccumulation/BR-response phenotypes. 2. ces-D accumulated increased late BR intermediates 6-DeoxoCS and CS, while DWF4, CPD and ROT3 transcripts were elevated. 3. ces-1 loss-of-function reduced hypocotyl elongation and lowered DWF4/ROT3 expression; BL rescued the phenotype. 4. CES-SRDX caused BR-deficient dwarf phenotypes reversible by 24-epiBL. 5. CES directly binds G-box motifs in CPD and CYP718 promoters. 6. CES localizes to the nucleus, interacts with BEE1 in vivo, and is phosphorylated by BIN2 in vitro.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating CES or its regulatory module could tune endogenous brassinosteroid biosynthesis and growth, though potential pleiotropic developmental effects are substantial
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CES transcript abundance is not BL regulated, unlike BEE1; functional redundancy likely masks strong knockout phenotypes; DOI not present in supplied text.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix whole-genome Arabidopsis GeneChip, accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: brassinosteroids control plant growth, development, hypocotyl elongation, leaf morphology and meristem activity
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes prolonged vegetative growth, delayed flowering/senescence, altered leaf morphology and increased axillary shoot growth; repression causes dwarfism

Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus; relocalizes to distinct nuclear compartments after BL or Bikinin treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: BL inducible according to prior work cited
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested as YFP fusion in protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: NA in this paper; proposed to act with CES on BR-responsive genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: BR-responsive transcription; likely BR biosynthesis regulation as part of CES-BEE1 module
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: interacts with CES in yeast and in planta; colocalizes with CES in BR-induced nuclear compartments
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports CES regulatory module linked to positive regulation of BR responses/biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BEE1 is a close CES homolog and positive BR-response regulator from prior work. 2. In this study BEE1 colocalized with CES in BR/Bikinin-induced nuclear compartments. 3. CES interacted with BEE1 in yeast and in planta, supporting a CES-BEE1 regulatory module.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be co-targeted with CES to modulate BR outputs, but direct metabolic evidence in this paper is limited
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not the primary subject of experimentation for metabolite changes, but experimentally validated as part of the core CES mechanism.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: brassinosteroids regulate plant growth and development
